sentences.target: Ich habe seit gestern eine Erkältung.

translations.de: Ich habe seit gestern eine Erkältung.
translations.en: I’ve had a cold since yesterday.
translations.zh-Hans: 我从昨天开始就感冒了。
translations.yue: 我由尋日開始就感冒咗。
translations.ru: С вчерашнего дня у меня простуда.
translations.vi: Tôi bị cảm từ hôm qua.
translations.nb: Jeg har vært forkjølet siden i går.
translations.sv: Jag har varit förkyld sedan i går.
translations.da: Jeg har været forkølet siden i går.
translations.ja: 昨日から風邪をひいています。

tokens.explanation: Personal pronoun, 1st person singular nominative; subject of the clause.
Main verb (present, 1st sg) expressing possession/state; governs the direct object 'eine Erkältung' and takes the temporal PP 'seit gestern' as an adjunct.
Preposition introducing a temporal phrase marking the starting point ('since'); here takes the adverb 'gestern' as its complement.
Temporal adverb functioning as the complement to 'seit', specifying the start time.
Indefinite article, feminine singular accusative; determiner for 'Erkältung' as direct object.
Common noun, feminine; accusative direct object of 'habe'.

translations.en: My cold is slowly getting better.
translations.es: Mi resfriado está mejorando poco a poco.
translations.fr: Mon rhume s'améliore petit à petit.
translations.it: Il mio raffreddore sta migliorando pian piano.
translations.zh-Hans: 我的感冒在慢慢好起来。
translations.pt: Meu resfriado está melhorando aos poucos.
translations.vi: Cơn cảm của tôi đang dần đỡ hơn.
translations.hi: मेरा जुकाम धीरे-धीरे ठीक हो रहा है.
translations.el: Το κρυολόγημά μου περνάει σιγά σιγά.
translations.ja: 風邪が少しずつよくなってきています。

tokens.explanation: Possessive determiner, feminine singular nominative; modifies 'Erkältung' (subject).
Common noun, feminine; nominative subject of the clause.
Auxiliary (copular use) in present, 3rd sg; links the subject to the predicate adjective 'besser'.
Adverb of manner/modality; modifies the copular construction, indicating gradual improvement.
Predicate adjective, comparative of 'gut'; complements the copula 'wird'.

sentences.target: Tom holt sich im Zug immer eine Erkältung.
sentences.reading: /tɔm hoːlt zɪç ɪm tsuːk ˈɪmɐ ˈaɪ̯nə ɛɐ̯ˈkɛltʊŋ/

translations.de: Tom holt sich im Zug immer eine Erkältung.
translations.ipa: /tɔm hoːlt zɪç ɪm tsuːk ˈɪmɐ ˈaɪ̯nə ɛɐ̯ˈkɛltʊŋ/
translations.en: Tom always catches a cold on the train.
translations.es: Tom siempre se resfría en el tren.
translations.fr: Tom attrape toujours un rhume dans le train.
translations.it: Tom prende sempre il raffreddore in treno.
translations.ko: 톰은 기차를 타면 항상 감기에 걸려요.
translations.zh-Hans: 汤姆坐火车时总是会感冒。
translations.yue: 湯姆每次坐火車都會感冒。
translations.pt: Tom sempre fica resfriado no trem.
translations.vi: Tom lúc nào cũng bị cảm trên tàu.
translations.ar: توم دائمًا يُصاب بالزكام في القطار.
translations.nl: Tom wordt altijd verkouden in de trein.
translations.hi: टॉम को हमेशा ट्रेन में जुकाम हो जाता है.
translations.pl: Tom zawsze się przeziębia w pociągu.
translations.el: Ο Τομ κρυολογεί πάντα στο τρένο.
translations.nb: Tom blir alltid forkjølet på toget.
translations.id: Tom selalu masuk angin di kereta.
translations.sv: Tom blir alltid förkyld på tåget.
translations.cs: Tom se vždy nachladí ve vlaku.
translations.da: Tom bliver altid forkølet i toget.
translations.ja: トムは電車に乗るといつも風邪をひきます。

tokens.explanation: Proper name; nominative subject of the clause.
Main verb (present, 3rd sg); with reflexive 'sich' forms the idiomatic expression 'sich eine Erkältung holen' ('to catch a cold').
Reflexive pronoun (3rd person, dative), coreferential with the subject; part of the reflexive construction with 'holt'.
Preposition (contracted form of 'in dem'); introduces a locative PP ('im Zug'), assigning dative to its object.
Common noun, masculine; object of the preposition 'im' (dative singular), indicating location.
Frequency adverb; modifies the verb phrase, indicating habitual action.
Indefinite article, feminine singular accusative; determiner for 'Erkältung' as direct object.
Common noun, feminine; accusative direct object within the reflexive verb construction.

sentences.target: Ich brauche Tee gegen die Erkältung.

translations.de: Ich brauche Tee gegen die Erkältung.
translations.en: I need tea for my cold.
translations.fr: J'ai besoin de thé pour mon rhume.
translations.it: Ho bisogno di tè per il raffreddore.
translations.zh-Hans: 我需要喝茶来缓解感冒。
translations.yue: 我需要飲茶嚟對付感冒。
translations.pt: Preciso de chá para o meu resfriado.
translations.vi: Tôi cần trà để trị cảm.
translations.nl: Ik heb thee nodig tegen mijn verkoudheid.
translations.hu: Teára van szükségem a megfázás ellen.
translations.hi: मुझे जुकाम के लिए चाय चाहिए.
translations.id: Saya butuh teh untuk meredakan masuk angin.
translations.ro: Am nevoie de ceai pentru răceală.
translations.da: Jeg har brug for te mod forkølelsen.
translations.ja: 風邪に効くお茶が必要です。

tokens.explanation: Personal pronoun, 1st person singular nominative; subject of the clause.
Main verb (present, 1st sg); takes 'Tee' as accusative direct object and a PP 'gegen die Erkältung' indicating purpose/benefit.
Common noun, masculine; accusative direct object of 'brauche'.
Preposition selecting accusative; introduces a PP expressing opposition/purpose ('against the cold').
Definite article, feminine singular accusative; determiner for 'Erkältung' as object of 'gegen'.
Common noun, feminine; accusative object of the preposition 'gegen'.

Ich brauche Tee gegen die Erkältung.

Tom always catches a cold on the train.
